content: schwab-py client_from_access_functions takes (api_key, app_secret, token_read_func, token_write_func, asyncio=False). token_read_func takes no args, returns the wrapped token dict. token_write_func is called with the wrapped dict on every refresh. The wrapped dict shape is exactly: {'creation_timestamp': int_unix_ts, 'token': <oauth2_dict>}. The inner oauth2_dict contains authlib OAuth2 fields (access_token, refresh_token, expires_at, token_type, scope). TokenMetadata.from_loaded_token() raises ValueError if 'creation_timestamp' missing. The lib recommends storing the entire wrapped dict as opaque JSON. Two separate client_from_access_functions calls with distinct read/write funcs is the intended pattern for two apps.
